title: September 2022 — 'when in doubt in soil science, add organic matter'
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only September 2022 study heuristic: "When in doubt on a soil science (likely SOIL 205/206) question, the answer is 'add organic matter.'"
  • A when in doubt heuristic — Yahir noticing exam-question pattern matching, not just learning content.
  • Adds soil science to the osu coursework entity graph — a rare in-vault datapoint on Yahir's osu earth systems / soil-related coursework before/during the cs major pivot.
  • Sister to 2022 09 30 look into switching insurance (both fall-2022 osu coursework / soil-lab notes) and 2022 04 21 water treatment coursework / 2022 04 21 water treatment wetlands (spring environmental coursework).
  • Reads as Yahir doing the exam meta move that Karpathy-style metalearning would later formalize for him — pattern-recognize the test before optimizing the studying.

What's actually in the note

(Body empty — content is the title.)

when I'm in doubt in. SOIL SCIENCE put "add organic matter"

Why it matters

Three small graph payoffs:

  1. Coursework dating. Adds a fall-2022 soil science class to Yahir's transcript-as-implied-by-vault. Likely SOIL 205 or SOIL 206.
  2. Heuristic-spotting. The "when in doubt → default answer" frame is a test-taking heuristic — the same epistemic move he later uses for product decisions (when in doubt heuristic).
  3. Earth-systems chapter. Confirms Yahir was still taking earth/soil coursework as late as fall 2022 — meaning the cs major pivot (started spring 2022 per 2022 03 28 talk to marcella arch hours) didn't immediately end the natural-resources coursework.

Open questions

  • Did "add organic matter" actually work as a test heuristic? (No follow-up grade data; he's still taking the class through the term.)
  • Earth Systems major or just gen-ed? (Unknown from this note alone; later 2023 notes might disambiguate.)
